qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) in workflow import from URL endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes SSRF in the workflow import endpoint at GET /workflows/from-url, while the advisory describes prototype pollution in the GSuiteAdmin and XML nodes leading to RCE. These are different vulnerability classes (CWE-918 vs CWE-1321) affecting different components with different root causes. The SSRF vulnerability is in a different API endpoint and does not involve prototype pollution.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The qualifying finding does not identify the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes prototype pollution (CWE-1321) in the GSuiteAdmin and XML nodes that leads to remote code execution. The qualifying finding describes SSRF in a workflow import endpoint (CWE-918). This affects a different component and has a different root cause than the advisory vulnerability.
evidence: Advisory: Prototype pollution in GSuiteAdmin/XML nodes → RCE (CWE-1321)
Finding: SSRF via GET /workflows/from-url (CWE-918) - different vulnerability class and affected component
No overlap in vulnerable functions, endpoints, or root-cause mechanisms between the advisory and the qualifying finding
